message:
feat: in-place upgrade smoke workflow (#8721)

* feat: in-place upgrade smoke workflow (C.3 offload)

Add a CI workflow that exercises the console's self-upgrade mechanism
end-to-end: installs baseline via Helm on Kind, triggers
/api/self-upgrade/trigger with a HEAD-built image, polls the Deployment
spec for rollover, and verifies the upgraded pod is healthy.

Runs on schedule (every 6h) and on PRs touching the upgrade handler,
Helm chart, Dockerfile, or upgrade UI components.

This is the gold-standard canary for in-place upgrade — if it fails on
main, every deployed console instance's upgrade path is broken.

Covers the C.3 gap in the reviewer offload catalog.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: use --set-string for DEV_MODE env value in upgrade smoke

Helm --set passes 'true' as a boolean, but Kubernetes EnvVar .value
requires a string. Use --set-string to ensure the value is quoted.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: replace sleep-then-curl with health poll loop in upgrade smoke

The second CI run failed with exit code 22 (connection reset) because
the Go binary inside the container hadn't finished starting within the
10s PORT_FORWARD_SETTLE_S window. Replace the single sleep+curl with
a poll loop that retries every HEALTH_POLL_INTERVAL_S (5s) up to
HEALTH_TIMEOUT_S (120s), matching the pattern used in auth-login-smoke.yml.

Applied to both the baseline health check and the post-upgrade check.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: add GHCR login for baseline image pull

Runs 1-3 all failed. Most likely cause: docker pull of
ghcr.io/kubestellar/console:latest without authentication, which
fails silently (|| true) and causes kind load to fail on a
nonexistent local image.

Fix:
- Add docker/login-action@v3 for ghcr.io using GITHUB_TOKEN
- Remove || true on docker pull so failures are caught immediately

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: use /health (no auth) + dev-mode cookie for /api/* routes

Root cause of runs 1-4 failure: /api/health and /api/self-upgrade/*
sit behind auth middleware that requires a JWT even in DEV_MODE.
The curl calls were unauthenticated, so every request returned 401
'missing authorization' — which curl -sf reports as exit code 22.

Fix:
- Health polling uses /health (outside auth middleware, no JWT needed)
- Add 'Get dev-mode auth cookie' step: GET /auth/github in DEV_MODE
  auto-creates a dev-user and returns a kc_auth HttpOnly cookie
- All /api/* calls now pass -b COOKIE_JAR for authentication
- Matches the pattern used by auth-login-smoke.yml

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: pass deployment name to SSAR so resourceNames-scoped Role matches

The self-upgrade Role uses resourceNames to restrict patch access to
the console's own Deployment only. But canPatchDeployment() was calling
SelfSubjectAccessReview without a Name field, which checks 'can I patch
ANY deployment?' — the scoped Role correctly denies this broader ask.

Fix: pass the discovered deployment name to canPatchDeployment() so the
SSAR includes Name in ResourceAttributes, matching the Role's scope.

This was caught by the upgrade-smoke CI workflow (run 5): the pod
started successfully, auth worked, but canPatch returned false with
'insufficient RBAC' because the SSAR/Role mismatch.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: use HEAD-built image as baseline to include SSAR fix

The baseline :latest image has the old canPatchDeployment() code that
doesn't pass the deployment name to the SSAR — so canPatch always
returns false with the resourceNames-scoped Role. Since we fixed the
Go code in this PR, the :ci-head image has the fix but the :latest
baseline does not.

Fix: build HEAD once, tag as both :ci-baseline and :ci-head. Install
with :ci-baseline, upgrade to :ci-head. Both tags use the same binary
so the SSAR fix is present from the start. The upgrade still tests
the full mechanism (tag change triggers Deployment rollout).

Future improvement: once the SSAR fix is released, switch baseline
back to the latest published release to test cross-version upgrades.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix(auth): dev-mode user gets admin role for full feature access

Dev-mode users were created with the default viewer role, which
prevented testing admin-only features like self-upgrade trigger,
settings management, and RBAC operations. Since dev mode exists
for testing, the dev user should have admin access to exercise
all features.

This also fixes the upgrade-smoke CI workflow which requires
admin role to POST /api/self-upgrade/trigger.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix(upgrade-smoke): capture HTTP error body, add admin role check

Run 8 failed with exit 22 at trigger step but curl -sf suppressed
the error response body. Changed to curl -sS -o file -w http_code
to capture the full response regardless of HTTP status.

Also added a 'Verify dev user is admin' step before the trigger to
confirm the dev-mode admin role fix is working correctly.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix(upgrade-smoke): add CSRF header to trigger request

The /api/* group requires X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest on all
state-changing requests (POST/PUT/DELETE). The trigger curl was
missing this header, causing a 403 'CSRF header required' error.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,260 @@
+# In-place upgrade smoke test
+#
+# Verifies the console's self-upgrade mechanism end-to-end:
+#   1. Build HEAD image, tag as :ci-baseline and :ci-head
+#   2. Create a Kind cluster, install :ci-baseline via Helm
+#   3. Get dev-mode auth cookie, confirm canPatch=true
+#   4. POST /api/self-upgrade/trigger with imageTag=ci-head
+#   5. Poll the Deployment spec until the image tag rolls to :ci-head
+#   6. Verify the upgraded pod is healthy (/health returns 200)
+#
+# This is the gold-standard canary for the self-upgrade mechanism.
+# If this workflow fails on main, every deployed console instance's
+# upgrade path is potentially broken.
+#
+# Does NOT duplicate:
+#   - auth-login-smoke.yml (OAuth contract, hourly)
+#   - fullstack-e2e.yml (Playwright on PR)
+#   - startup-smoke.yml (Docker entrypoint, daily)
+
+name: In-Place Upgrade Smoke
+
+on:
+  pull_request:
+    paths:
+      - .github/workflows/upgrade-smoke.yml
+      - pkg/api/handlers/self_upgrade.go
+      - pkg/api/server.go
+      - deploy/helm/kubestellar-console/**
+      - Dockerfile
+      - cmd/console/**
+      - web/src/hooks/useSelfUpgrade.ts
+      - web/src/hooks/useVersionCheck.tsx
+      - web/src/components/settings/UpdateSettings.tsx
+  schedule:
+    # Every 6 hours, minute 17 to avoid :00 congestion
+    - cron: '17 */6 * * *'
+  workflow_dispatch: {}
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+  packages: read
+
+concurrency:
+  group: upgrade-smoke-${{ github.ref }}
+  cancel-in-progress: true
+
+env:
+  RELEASE_NAME: console
+  NAMESPACE: upgrade-smoke
+  TARGET_TAG: ci-head
+  BASELINE_TAG: ci-baseline
+  IMAGE_REPO: ghcr.io/kubestellar/console
+  # Timeouts (seconds) — named constants, no magic numbers
+  HEALTH_POLL_INTERVAL_S: 5
+  HEALTH_TIMEOUT_S: 120
+  UPGRADE_TIMEOUT_S: 300
+  PORT_FORWARD_SETTLE_S: 10
+  HELM_INSTALL_TIMEOUT: 5m
+  ROLLOUT_TIMEOUT: 5m
+
+jobs:
+  smoke:
+    if: github.repository == 'kubestellar/console'
+    name: In-Place Upgrade Smoke
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 25
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
+
+      - name: Login to GHCR
+        uses: docker/login-action@v3
+        with:
+          registry: ghcr.io
+          username: ${{ github.actor }}
+          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+
+      - name: Build HEAD image and tag as baseline + target
+        run: |
+          # Build once, tag twice. Both tags use the same binary so the
+          # SSAR fix is present in the baseline. The upgrade changes the
+          # Deployment's image tag from :ci-baseline to :ci-head, proving
+          # the rollout mechanism works.
+          docker build -t ${{ env.IMAGE_REPO }}:${{ env.TARGET_TAG }} .
+          docker tag ${{ env.IMAGE_REPO }}:${{ env.TARGET_TAG }} \
+                     ${{ env.IMAGE_REPO }}:${{ env.BASELINE_TAG }}
+
+      - name: Create Kind cluster
+        uses: helm/kind-action@v1
+        with:
+          cluster_name: upgrade-smoke
+          wait: 60s
+
+      - name: Load images into Kind
+        run: |
+          kind load docker-image ${{ env.IMAGE_REPO }}:${{ env.BASELINE_TAG }} --name upgrade-smoke
+          kind load docker-image ${{ env.IMAGE_REPO }}:${{ env.TARGET_TAG }} --name upgrade-smoke
+
+      - name: Install baseline via Helm
+        run: |
+          kubectl create namespace ${{ env.NAMESPACE }}
+          helm install ${{ env.RELEASE_NAME }} ./deploy/helm/kubestellar-console \
+            -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} \
+            --set image.repository=${{ env.IMAGE_REPO }} \
+            --set image.tag=${{ env.BASELINE_TAG }} \
+            --set image.pullPolicy=IfNotPresent \
+            --set 'extraEnv[0].name=DEV_MODE' \
+            --set-string 'extraEnv[0].value=true' \
+            --wait --timeout=${{ env.HELM_INSTALL_TIMEOUT }}
+
+      - name: Port-forward and verify baseline health
+        run: |
+          kubectl -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} port-forward \
+            svc/${{ env.RELEASE_NAME }}-kubestellar-console 8080:8080 &
+          PF_PID=$!
+          echo "PF_PID=$PF_PID" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
+
+          # Poll /health (no auth required) until the backend is up.
+          # /api/* routes require a JWT even in DEV_MODE; /health does not.
+          DEADLINE=$(( $(date +%s) + ${{ env.HEALTH_TIMEOUT_S }} ))
+          echo "Waiting for /health (timeout: ${{ env.HEALTH_TIMEOUT_S }}s)..."
+          while [ "$(date +%s)" -lt "$DEADLINE" ]; do
+            if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8080/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
+              echo "Backend healthy after $(( $(date +%s) - DEADLINE + ${{ env.HEALTH_TIMEOUT_S }} ))s"
+              break
+            fi
+            sleep ${{ env.HEALTH_POLL_INTERVAL_S }}
+          done
+
+          echo "--- /health ---"
+          curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8080/health | jq .
+
+      - name: Get dev-mode auth cookie
+        run: |
+          # DEV_MODE auto-creates a dev-user on /auth/github and returns
+          # a kc_auth HttpOnly cookie. All /api/* routes require this JWT.
+          COOKIE_JAR=/tmp/cookies.txt
+          curl -sS --max-time 10 \
+            -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
+            -o /dev/null \
+            http://127.0.0.1:8080/auth/github
+          echo "COOKIE_JAR=$COOKIE_JAR" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
+
+          # Verify cookie was set
+          if ! grep -q 'kc_auth' "$COOKIE_JAR"; then
+            echo "FAIL: dev-mode login did not set kc_auth cookie"
+            cat "$COOKIE_JAR"
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          echo "Got dev-mode auth cookie"
+
+      - name: Verify dev user is admin
+        run: |
+          echo "--- /api/me (user role check) ---"
+          ME=$(curl -sS -b "$COOKIE_JAR" http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/me)
+          echo "$ME" | jq '{login: .githubLogin, role: .role, id: .id}'
+
+          echo "$ME" | jq -e '.role == "admin"' \
+            || { echo "FAIL: dev user role is not admin — got $(echo "$ME" | jq -r .role)"; exit 1; }
+          echo "Dev user has admin role"
+
+      - name: Verify self-upgrade status
+        run: |
+          echo "--- /api/self-upgrade/status ---"
+          STATUS=$(curl -sS -b "$COOKIE_JAR" http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/self-upgrade/status)
+          echo "$STATUS" | jq .
+
+          # Verify canPatch is true — RBAC is working
+          echo "$STATUS" | jq -e '.canPatch == true' \
+            || { echo "FAIL: canPatch is not true — selfUpgrade RBAC may be broken"; exit 1; }
+
+      - name: Trigger upgrade to HEAD
+        run: |
+          echo "Triggering upgrade to tag: ${{ env.TARGET_TAG }}"
+          HTTP_CODE=$(curl -sS -o /tmp/trigger_response.json -w '%{http_code}' \
+            -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/self-upgrade/trigger \
+            -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
+            -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
+            -H 'X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest' \
+            -d '{"imageTag":"${{ env.TARGET_TAG }}"}')
+          echo "HTTP $HTTP_CODE"
+          cat /tmp/trigger_response.json | jq . 2>/dev/null || cat /tmp/trigger_response.json
+
+          if [ "$HTTP_CODE" -ge 400 ]; then
+            echo "FAIL: trigger returned HTTP $HTTP_CODE"
+            exit 1
+          fi
+
+          jq -e '.success == true' /tmp/trigger_response.json \
+            || { echo "FAIL: trigger returned success=false"; jq -r '.error // .message // "no detail"' /tmp/trigger_response.json; exit 1; }
+
+      - name: Poll Deployment image until rollover to :ci-head
+        run: |
+          DEPLOY="${{ env.RELEASE_NAME }}-kubestellar-console"
+          DEADLINE=$(( $(date +%s) + ${{ env.UPGRADE_TIMEOUT_S }} ))
+
+          echo "Waiting for Deployment $DEPLOY image to roll to :${{ env.TARGET_TAG }}"
+          while [ "$(date +%s)" -lt "$DEADLINE" ]; do
+            IMG=$(kubectl -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} get deployment "$DEPLOY" \
+              -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}')
+            echo "  $(date +%H:%M:%S) image=$IMG"
+            case "$IMG" in
+              *:"${{ env.TARGET_TAG }}")
+                echo "Image rolled to target tag"
+                break
+                ;;
+            esac
+            sleep ${{ env.HEALTH_POLL_INTERVAL_S }}
+          done
+
+          # Wait for rollout to complete (new pods ready)
+          kubectl -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} rollout status \
+            "deployment/$DEPLOY" --timeout=${{ env.ROLLOUT_TIMEOUT }}
+
+      - name: Verify upgraded pod is healthy
+        run: |
+          # Port-forward may have died during rollout — restart it
+          kill "$PF_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
+          sleep 2
+          kubectl -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} port-forward \
+            svc/${{ env.RELEASE_NAME }}-kubestellar-console 8080:8080 &
+
+          # Poll /health (no auth) until the upgraded pod responds
+          DEADLINE=$(( $(date +%s) + ${{ env.HEALTH_TIMEOUT_S }} ))
+          echo "Waiting for post-upgrade /health (timeout: ${{ env.HEALTH_TIMEOUT_S }}s)..."
+          while [ "$(date +%s)" -lt "$DEADLINE" ]; do
+            if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8080/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
+              echo "Upgraded pod healthy after $(( $(date +%s) - DEADLINE + ${{ env.HEALTH_TIMEOUT_S }} ))s"
+              break
+            fi
+            sleep ${{ env.HEALTH_POLL_INTERVAL_S }}
+          done
+
+          echo "--- Post-upgrade /health ---"
+          curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8080/health | jq .
+
+          echo "--- Post-upgrade /api/self-upgrade/status ---"
+          curl -sf -b "$COOKIE_JAR" http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/self-upgrade/status | jq .
+
+          echo "Upgrade smoke test PASSED"
+
+      - name: Dump diagnostics on failure
+        if: failure()
+        run: |
+          echo "=== Namespace resources ==="
+          kubectl -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} get all
+          echo ""
+          echo "=== Deployment describe ==="
+          kubectl -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} describe \
+            deployment/${{ env.RELEASE_NAME }}-kubestellar-console
+          echo ""
+          echo "=== Pod logs (tail 500) ==="
+          kubectl -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} logs \
+            deployment/${{ env.RELEASE_NAME }}-kubestellar-console \
+            --tail=500 || true
+          echo ""
+          echo "=== Events ==="
+          kubectl -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} get events \
+            --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -30
